mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2022 payrolls at 151314k revised to 151623k showing strong labor demand
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS changes falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the positive return hit rate during payroll growth months is less than 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: An effective federal funds rate above 2.0 percent indicates a restrictive monetary stance that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed began hiking in March 2022 with April effective rate at 0.33 percent
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS above 2.0 percent, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative via t-test at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly high consumer price inflation and low unemployment force accelerated monetary tightening; the interaction of CPIAUCSL and UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as real rates rise sharply.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2022 CPI at 288.6 and unemployment at 3.6 percent
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and UNRATE produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal yields positive returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth during late-cycle expansions signals resilient consumer spending that supports revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2022 retail sales at 667724M revised down to 651027M indicating potential deceleration
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansion months, positive return hit rate is below 55 percent, or mean 5-day forward return is not significantly positive via t-test at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and downward revenue revisions, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with negative RSAFS growth show a negative return hit rate below 55 percent, or mean 20-day forward return is not significantly negative via t-test at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth indicates improving supply-side output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2022 industrial production index at 104.6 revised down to 101.4
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO changes remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency over 60-day horizons is less than 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market momentum, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2022 unemployment at 3.6 percent revised to 3.7 percent
failure_condition: Triggered periods produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative via t-test at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a rising but still low federal funds rate compresses real equity discount rates and signals policy catch-up risk, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2022 CPI at 288.6 with Fed funds at 0.33 percent
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
